front: Work: involuntary memories triggered by eating a tea-soaked madeleine, bringing back memories of Combray
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: C.K. Scott Moncrieff's English translation was titled 'Remembrance of Things Past' from Shakespeare's Sonnet 30
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: the fictional composer Vinteuil's 'little phrase' in a violin sonata symbolizes Swann's love for Odette de Crecy
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: the Baron de Charlus has a homosexual encounter with the tailor Jupien; narrator observes while watching a bee fertilize an orchid
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: opens with an 'overture' beginning 'for a long time I went to bed early'; narrator cannot sleep without mother's goodnight kiss
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: two lovers use the phrase 'to do a cattleya' as a euphemism for sex after rearranging a corsage
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: the writer Bergotte dies in front of Vermeer's View of Delft, wishing he had written like the 'patch of yellow wall'
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: competing theories that Vinteuil's sonata is modeled on Franck, Brahms, Saint-Saens, or a Gabriel Perne D Minor Sonata
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: a kitchen-maid is nicknamed 'Giotto's Charity'; a woman resembles Botticelli's Zipporah
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: a character receives a magic lantern that projects images of children's books and distorts his bedroom
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: Robert de Montesquiou was the real-life basis for the Baron de Charlus
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: the narrator compares a 'neurasthenic' relative to a water lily floating back and forth across a river
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: Bloch attends a dinner and badgers Madame de Norpois about the Dreyfus Affair
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: the narrator tramples a possibly homosexual character's new silk hat after being accused of spreading lies
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: the narrator is obsessed with the actress Berma, who performs in Racine plays; he is let down by her acting in Volume 2
back: In Search of Lost Time (Marcel Proust)

front: Work: this character dies in a horse-riding accident; her 'disappearance' titles The Fugitive, the sixth volume
back: Albertine Simonet (In Search of Lost Time, Marcel Proust)

front: Work: the narrator jealously imprisons this character in his house over her lesbian affairs in The Prisoner
back: Albertine Simonet (In Search of Lost Time, Marcel Proust)

front: Work: the narrator first meets this character on a beach at Balbec in Within a Budding Grove
back: Albertine Simonet (In Search of Lost Time, Marcel Proust)

front: Work: Gide's 'transposition theory' holds that this character is actually male, based on Proust's chauffeur or secretary
back: Albertine Simonet (In Search of Lost Time, Marcel Proust)

front: Work: Anne Carson's 'Albertine Workout' notes this character's name occurs 2,363 times; on 19% of pages she is asleep
back: Albertine Simonet (In Search of Lost Time, Marcel Proust)

front: Work: Proust attacked the biographically-oriented literary criticism of Charles Augustin Sainte-Beuve in this essay
back: Against Sainte-Beuve (Marcel Proust)

front: Author: lover of the Venezuelan composer Reynaldo Hahn
back: Marcel Proust

front: Author: Jozef Czapski delivered lectures on this author in a Soviet prison camp
back: Marcel Proust

front: Author: Alain de Botton wrote How [this author] Can Change Your Life
back: Marcel Proust

front: Author: a 'confession album' (namesake Questionnaire) he filled out at age 14; used by James Lipton; appears in Vanity Fair
back: Marcel Proust

front: Author: Samuel Beckett wrote a monograph on this author arguing he completely ignored moral considerations
back: Marcel Proust

front: Author: Andre Gide initially rejected this author's manuscript at the NRF
back: Marcel Proust

front: Author: lived in a cork-lined room in his last years
back: Marcel Proust

front: Author: early autobiographical novel Jean Santeuil was later expanded into In Search of Lost Time
back: Marcel Proust

front: Author: Anatole France wrote a foreword to this author's collection Pleasures and Days
back: Marcel Proust

front: Author: this author's essay 'On Reading' discusses his translation of Ruskin's Sesame and Lilies
back: Marcel Proust

front: Author: the narrator's mother reads him George Sand just before the famous madeleine episode
back: Marcel Proust

front: Author: In Fun Home, Alison Bechdel theorizes this author inspired her father's love of lilacs
back: Marcel Proust
